title: Delicate Fermented Ethiopian Shungiku Dulet
description: A fermented, cheese-pull, brunch-mood Ethiopian dish featuring shungiku, emulsify, kosher-friendly, in the form of dulet.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine chopped shungiku, onion, garlic, and ginger.
In a small bowl, mix together berbere spice, niter kibbeh, yogurt, salt, and black pepper.
Add the spice mixture to the shungiku mixture and stir until well combined.
Cover the bowl with a cloth and let it ferment at room temperature for 24 hours.
After fermentation, heat a non-stick pan over medium heat and add the fermented mixture.
Cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mixture is heated through and slightly caramelized.
Warm the injera bread by wrapping it in a damp cloth and microwaving for 20 seconds.
Assemble the dulet by placing a portion of the fermented mixture on each piece of injera bread and serving immediately.
